sections.paragraph:
ἀσώματος, ον (σῶμα; Pla. et al.; Philostrat., Vi. Apoll. 8, 18 p. 333, 26; Plotinus 1, 6, 6 al.; Herm. Wr. 2, 4b θεῖον ἢ θεός; Celsus 3, 32; PGM 4, 1777; ὁ ἀ. [Michael] TestAbr A 3 p. 79, 29 [Stone p. 6] al.; πνεύματα ἀ. TestSol 2:5 L; Philo; Just.; Tat. 25, 2; Ath.) bodiless δαιμόνιον (q.v. 2) ISm 3:2; w. δαιμονικός ISm 2.—TW. Sv.

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Philostrat Philostrat = Flavius Philostratus, the Sophist II–III a.d.—List 5

Vi. Vi. = Vita, Vitae

Plotinus Plotinus , III a.d.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Herm. Wr. Herm. Wr. = Hermetic Writings—List 5

Celsus Celsus , in Origen, Contra Celsum, II a.d.—List 5

PGM PGM = Papyri Graecae Magicae—List 4

TestAbr TestAbr = Testament of Abraham, with some interpolations; I b.c./I a.d.—Lists 2, 5

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

q.v. q.v. = quod vide (which see, see that which has just been mentioned)

ISm ISm = Ignatius to the Smyrnaeans, I–II a.d.—List 1

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Sv Sv (at the end of entries)=HSieben, Voces—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 147
